seo.title: Tri-Cities Senior & Family Photographer | It’s A Keeper
seo.description: Tri-Cities senior, family, couples, newborn and branding photographer in Richland. Outdoor golden-hour sessions with Lisa.

hero.imageAlt: A couple sharing a quiet embrace beneath autumn trees during a golden-hour session in Richland
hero.heading: Tri-Cities Portraits In Warm Light

why.paragraph: I’ll guide you when you need it, then leave room for the laugh, the hand squeeze, and the look that happens on its own. We’ll choose a Tri-Cities location that feels like you and follow the light together.
why.rearImageAlt: A high school senior standing among warm autumn leaves in the Tri-Cities
why.frontImageAlt: A couple holding their baby close in golden autumn light near Richland

cards.label: Seniors
cards.imageAlt: High school senior in a black dress photographed at golden hour in Richland

cards.label: Families
cards.imageAlt: A family laughing together during an outdoor Tri-Cities portrait session

cards.label: Couples
cards.imageAlt: A couple sharing an easy outdoor moment near Richland, Washington

cards.label: Newborns
cards.imageAlt: A mother kissing her newborn during a gentle lifestyle session in Richland

photoBreak.imageAlt: Parents holding their young child in glowing Columbia Basin evening light
photoBreak.scriptPhrase: the light moves; the feeling stays
photoBreak.srHeading: Golden-hour family photography in the Tri-Cities

meetLisa.story: I grew up in Wisconsin and made the Tri-Cities home in 2005. Photography became personal after my daughter faced early health challenges; it taught me to notice how quickly the smallest, most ordinary moments become the ones we miss.
meetLisa.credit: My camera has taken me to a session with a Grammy winner and assignments for a local magazine, but the heart of the work is still the person standing in front of me.
meetLisa.portraitAlt: Lisa, owner of It’s A Keeper Photography, holding her camera in the Tri-Cities

howItWorks.heading: A little care before the light
howItWorks.intro: I reserve most sessions four to six weeks ahead so we have time to plan it right.

steps.title: You reach out
steps.sentence: Tell me who you want to photograph and what this season means to you.

steps.title: We talk
steps.sentence: We’ll plan by phone, with wardrobe guidance and location ideas chosen for you.

steps.title: Golden hour
steps.sentence: We meet outdoors, move gently, and let natural connection do most of the work.

steps.title: Your gallery
steps.sentence: I edit each frame in my warm, true-to-you style and send your story home.

inquiry.heading: Let’s plan your golden hour
inquiry.scriptLine: the light is already on its way...
inquiry.supportLine: This takes less than a minute. I read and answer every inquiry myself.
inquiry.backgroundImageAlt: A senior standing in a sunlit Tri-Cities field near the end of the day

stepSession.question: Who’s in front of the camera?

stepSeason.question: What season do you picture?
stepSeason.note: I book about four to six weeks ahead, so there’s time to plan it right.

options.title: Somewhere outdoors and golden
options.detail: Open sky, warm light, and room to move.

options.title: A place that’s special to us
options.detail: Bring me somewhere that already holds a memory.
options.value: A place that’s special to us

options.title: Help me choose
options.detail: I’ll scout the light and share the places I know.
options.value: Help me choose — Lisa knows all the spots

stepStory.question: Tell me about you
stepStory.fieldLabel: Who are these photos for? What moment are you hoping to keep?
stepStory.placeholder: Tell me who’s in this season with you, and what you never want to forget.

stepContact.question: Where should I reach you?
stepContact.privacyNote: No newsletters, no pressure—just me, calling to plan everything with you.

confirmation.title: Your note is with Lisa
confirmation.message: I read every inquiry myself and will reach out within two business days.
